figures.caption: Fig. 3. Schematic of the Early Fusion architecture. The number of feature maps corresponds to the filter count for each layer (8, 16, 32). Note that in the first layer, the IR (red) and ToF (green) feature maps are visually separated to illustrate the logical independence enforced by grouped convolutions; however, in deployment, they constitute a single contiguous tensor.

Fig. 4. Confusion matrices on the test set. Values are reported in percentages (%). The global layout highlights the superior disambiguation capability of the Early Fusion (right) compared to IR (left) and ToF (center), particularly for the ’Stop Inv’ class.

Fig. 5. Latent space visualization using t-SNE projections of the test set embeddings. The plots display the feature distribution for IR-only (left), ToF-only (center), and Early Fusion (right) configurations.
Fig. 6. On-device inference latency (left) and mean active power (right) across fusion strategies, both shown with logarithmic y-axes.
